award: Financial Times / Standard Chartered Business Book of the Year Award
best_category: Business book / economics and global affairs
category_fit: High: the book's AI infrastructure, platform economics, labor, compute, and strategy spine is naturally legible to a business-book jury.
current_status_as_of_2026_05_26: Open/near-open 2026 window: Standard Chartered says Business Book of the Year submissions close June 30 and shortlist is announced in autumn.
eligibility_window_or_timing: 2026 cycle; spring opening; June 30 close; London winner event at end of year.
required_format_or_submission_materials: Publisher-ready entry package, final or near-final book files, commercial metadata, author/publisher contact, and physical/digital review materials as requested by current entry portal.
editorial_design_standard: Needs finished trade-book polish, fact-tight economics chapters, design-grade figures, and a jacket/positioning package that reads as business writing rather than technical notes.
review_blurb_need: Needs at least four business/technology/economics blurbs plus 10-15 serious early trade reviews or endorsements before entry.
submission_calendar_action: Do not enter 2026 unless hard manuscript gates close before June 20; otherwise place in 2027 watchlist and prepare by March 2027.
blocker: Current manuscript is below the final word-count invariant, has source-density gaps, visual-floor gaps, unsupported claims, and no final render QA.

category_fit: Medium-high if U.S. publication and author eligibility fit; stronger if framed as a deeply reported public-importance book, weaker if it remains a tech-industry explainer.
current_status_as_of_2026_05_26: 2027 cycle opens in June 2026 for books published in calendar 2026; final deadline listed as October 15, 2026, 5:30 p.m.
eligibility_window_or_timing: Books published in calendar 2026 for the 2027 Pulitzer cycle; early recommended deadline for Jan-Jul books is still TBD.
required_format_or_submission_materials: PDF no larger than 300 MB, $75 entry fee, eligible U.S.-published hardcover or bound paperback, and purchase availability by general public.
editorial_design_standard: Needs Pulitzer-grade literary nonfiction line-edit, verified public-interest argument, resolved permissions, and fully integrated citations/source notes.
review_blurb_need: Needs serious national review attention before/around publication; blurbs help but criticism/review ecosystem visibility matters more.
submission_calendar_action: If pursuing a 2026 publication, create Pulitzer account in June, hold July/TBD reminder, and final check by October 1; enter only after final PDF/print status is clean.
blocker: Eligibility, publisher route, public sale status, final PDF, and literary polish are unresolved.

category_fit: High prestige fit only if publication path is U.S. trade and the book reaches literary nonfiction standard; category fit is possible but demanding.
current_status_as_of_2026_05_26: 2026 guidelines require publisher delivery of submitted titles by June 5, 2026; this is effectively too soon for the current manuscript.
eligibility_window_or_timing: 2026 cycle: submissions opened in March; June 5 delivery/copy deadline referenced in guidelines.
required_format_or_submission_materials: Publisher submission, title copies or bound manuscript/galley by stated deadline, category-specific eligibility, and online entry form.
editorial_design_standard: Needs line-level literary quality, coherent narrative arc across all 24 chapters, and finished book design before judge copies.
review_blurb_need: Needs publisher-backed review/ARC plan and author-positioning materials; self-nomination path is not the primary lane.
submission_calendar_action: Skip 2026; if a U.S. trade publication path exists, build 2027 NBA readiness folder by February 2027 and verify guidelines in March.
blocker: No finished galley, no final design, no publisher-facing entry package, and key manuscript QA gates remain open.

category_fit: Medium-high: broad nonfiction and criticism ecosystem could recognize a polished AI-era public-knowledge book, but discoverability depends on critic attention.
current_status_as_of_2026_05_26: NBCC process page says it does not accept official submissions or charge fees; award recognizes books published in English in six categories.
eligibility_window_or_timing: Annual cycle around books published in the relevant year; finalists/winners occur after publication-year review season.
required_format_or_submission_materials: No standard paid entry; instead build critic outreach, finished copies/ARCs, public review availability, and discoverable metadata.
editorial_design_standard: Needs quotable prose, review-ready structure, clean fact apparatus, and a final copy that critics can read without production caveats.
review_blurb_need: Needs 30-50 critic/reviewer contacts, 15 serious ARC sends, and a publicist-style review calendar.
submission_calendar_action: After final production date, schedule critic outreach 4-5 months before publication and track follow-up through year-end.
blocker: No review pipeline, no final ARC, no publicist list, and the manuscript still reads as production-in-progress in several lanes.

award: Royal Society Trivedi Science Book Prize
category_fit: Medium: strong compute/AI science-and-technology fit, but business/history chapters must remain accessible and scientifically careful.
current_status_as_of_2026_05_26: 2026 call closed March 2, 2026, so current cycle is unavailable.
eligibility_window_or_timing: 2026 cycle closed March 2; monitor next call for 2027 and verify publisher/availability rules before entry.
required_format_or_submission_materials: Publisher/author entry through Royal Society form, eligibility declaration, and requested copies/files under current terms of entry.
editorial_design_standard: Needs popular-science clarity, careful handling of technical claims, strong diagrams, and non-specialist readability.
review_blurb_need: Needs science-writer/scientist blurbs and UK-facing availability/review support if eligible.
submission_calendar_action: Skip 2026; start 2027 monitoring in December 2026 and prepare package by January 2027.
blocker: 2026 window closed; current technical chapters still need source-density and visual repairs.

award: PEN/E.O. Wilson Literary Science Writing Award
category_fit: Medium: natural science category is narrower than AI/business history, but the book can compete if the science of computation is written with literary force and lay clarity.
current_status_as_of_2026_05_26: 2026 winners/longlists are already posted; future cycle must be checked when PEN opens submissions.
eligibility_window_or_timing: Annual PEN America cycle; verify next submission window and category-specific eligibility before planning publication date.
required_format_or_submission_materials: Publisher/eligible-submitter materials under PEN current guidelines; final published book, metadata, and eligibility documentation.
editorial_design_standard: Needs literary science-writing polish, fewer apparatus intrusions, and a stronger through-line from computation to human knowledge.
review_blurb_need: Needs science, literary, and technology blurbs rather than only industry endorsements.
submission_calendar_action: Add to 2027 watchlist; check PEN awards pages monthly from late summer 2026 until submissions reopen.
blocker: Current book is not yet literary-polished enough and eligibility route/window remain unsettled.

category_fit: Medium-high: science-and-technology and current-interest categories both fit a polished AI-era trade book.
current_status_as_of_2026_05_26: 2026 prizes recognize 2025 books; FAQ lists 13 categories including Science and Technology and Current Interest.
eligibility_window_or_timing: Annual cycle tied to previous year's books; verify 2027 submission mechanics after LA Times posts current rules.
required_format_or_submission_materials: Finished book/ARC and publisher/publicist submission materials under LA Times current process.
editorial_design_standard: Needs strong jacket, finished interior, clean visual credits, and a reading experience comparable to major trade titles.
review_blurb_need: Needs West Coast technology/media review outreach and festival/publicity readiness.
submission_calendar_action: Monitor after 2026 prize cycle; prepare candidate package only after final production schedule is known.
blocker: No final trade package, no ARC, and no review campaign.

award: Goodreads Choice / reader-choice and trade discovery lanes
best_category: Science and Technology / Nonfiction reader discovery
category_fit: Medium as commercial visibility, low as juried literary validation.
current_status_as_of_2026_05_26: Not a formal juried shortlist target; useful only after publication, reader traction, and metadata/category fit exist.
eligibility_window_or_timing: Post-publication reader discovery cycle; timing depends on platform rules and release date.
required_format_or_submission_materials: Final retail book, reader ratings/reviews, category metadata, and launch campaign assets.
editorial_design_standard: Needs cover, description, sample, price, and category tuning more than prize-submission polish.
review_blurb_need: Needs broad ARC/review engine and newsletter/social visibility.
submission_calendar_action: Use only after publication; do not let reader-award tactics replace manuscript QA or juried-award readiness.
blocker: No launch engine or final retail listing yet.
